channel.title: Recent Articles in Phys. Rev. Lett.
channel.description: Recent articles in Physical Review Letters
channel.dc:creator: rss@aps.org
channel.dc:rights: Copyright © 2026 the American Physical Society. Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited

item.title: Acoustoelectric Probing of Fractal Energy Spectra in Graphene/hBN Moiré Superlattices
item.description:
Author(s): Wenqing Song, Yicheng Mou, Qing Lan, Guorui Zhao, Zejing Guo, Jiaqi Liu, Tuoyu Zhao, Cheng Zhang, and Wu Shi

Moiré superlattices with long-range periodicity exhibit Hofstadter energy spectra under accessible magnetic fields, enabling the exploration of emergent quantum phenomena through a hierarchy of fractal states. However, higher-order features, located at elevated energies with narrow bandwidths, typic…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 036301] Published Wed Jan 21, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Wenqing Song, Yicheng Mou, Qing Lan, Guorui Zhao, Zejing Guo, Jiaqi Liu, Tuoyu Zhao, Cheng Zhang, and Wu Shi

Moiré superlattices with long-range periodicity exhibit Hofstadter energy spectra under accessible magnetic fields, enabling the exploration of emergent quantum phenomena through a hierarchy of fractal states. However, higher-order features, located at elevated energies with narrow bandwidths, typic…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 036301] Published Wed Jan 21, 2026
item.dc:title: Acoustoelectric Probing of Fractal Energy Spectra in Graphene/hBN Moiré Superlattices
item.dc:creator: Wenqing Song, Yicheng Mou, Qing Lan, Guorui Zhao, Zejing Guo, Jiaqi Liu, Tuoyu Zhao, Cheng Zhang, and Wu Shi

item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: ${\mathrm{FeTa}X}_{2}$: A Ferrimagnetic Quantum Anomalous Hall Insulator
item.description:
Author(s): Yadong Jiang, Huan Wang, and Jing Wang (王靖)

We propose the van der Waals layered ternary transition metal chalcogenides ${\mathrm{FeTa}X}_{2}$ ($X=\mathrm{S}$, Se, Te) as a new family of ferrimagnetic quantum anomalous Hall insulators with a sizable bulk gap and high Chern number $\mathcal{C}=−2$. The magnetic order arises primarily from Fe a…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 036601] Published Wed Jan 21, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Yadong Jiang, Huan Wang, and Jing Wang (王靖)

We propose the van der Waals layered ternary transition metal chalcogenides FeTaX2 (X=S, Se, Te) as a new family of ferrimagnetic quantum anomalous Hall insulators with a sizable bulk gap and high Chern number C=−2. The magnetic order arises primarily from Fe atoms, whose strong ferromagnetic exchan…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 036601] Published Wed Jan 21, 2026
item.dc:title: ${\mathrm{FeTa}X}_{2}$: A Ferrimagnetic Quantum Anomalous Hall Insulator
item.dc:creator: Yadong Jiang, Huan Wang, and Jing Wang (王靖)
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Fractal Spectrum in Twisted Bilayer Optical Lattice
item.description:
Author(s): Xu-Tao Wan, Chao Gao, and Zhe-Yu Shi

We investigate the full spectrum of twisted bilayer optical lattices (TBOLs) across all possible twist angles. Our calculation departs from the conventional moiré physics paradigm, which focuses on continuum theory at a fixed small twist angle. We discover that the full spectrum of a TBOL exhibits a…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 033401] Published Tue Jan 20, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Xu-Tao Wan, Chao Gao, and Zhe-Yu Shi

We investigate the full spectrum of twisted bilayer optical lattices (TBOLs) across all possible twist angles. Our calculation departs from the conventional moiré physics paradigm, which focuses on continuum theory at a fixed small twist angle. We discover that the full spectrum of a TBOL exhibits a…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 033401] Published Tue Jan 20, 2026
item.dc:title: Fractal Spectrum in Twisted Bilayer Optical Lattice
item.dc:creator: Xu-Tao Wan, Chao Gao, and Zhe-Yu Shi
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Atomic, Molecular, and Optical Physics

item.title: Gate-Tunable Spin Switching Effect and Bilinear Magnetoelectric Resistance in the Topological Semimetal
item.description:
Author(s): An-Qi Wang, Tong-Yang Zhao, Chuan Li, Xu-Dong Yang, Rui Zhu, Jing-Wei Dong, Alexander Brinkman, Chun-Guang Chu, and Zhi-Min Liao

Topological materials exhibit topologically protected surface states (TSS) characterized by helical spin texture and high charge-spin conversion efficiency, making them promising for high-performance spintronic devices. However, achieving gate control to switch between up and down spin-polarized sta…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 036201] Published Tue Jan 20, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): An-Qi Wang, Tong-Yang Zhao, Chuan Li, Xu-Dong Yang, Rui Zhu, Jing-Wei Dong, Alexander Brinkman, Chun-Guang Chu, and Zhi-Min Liao

Topological materials exhibit topologically protected surface states (TSS) characterized by helical spin texture and high charge-spin conversion efficiency, making them promising for high-performance spintronic devices. However, achieving gate control to switch between up and down spin-polarized sta…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 036201] Published Tue Jan 20, 2026
item.dc:title: Gate-Tunable Spin Switching Effect and Bilinear Magnetoelectric Resistance in the Topological Semimetal
item.dc:creator: An-Qi Wang, Tong-Yang Zhao, Chuan Li, Xu-Dong Yang, Rui Zhu, Jing-Wei Dong, Alexander Brinkman, Chun-Guang Chu, and Zhi-Min Liao
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Quarter-Metal Superconductivity in Rhombohedral Graphene
item.description:
Author(s): Chiho Yoon, Tianyi Xu, Yafis Barlas, and Fan Zhang

We investigate the recently discovered multiple superconducting states in rhombohedral graphene quarter metal. We demonstrate that one of these states features a single-spin, single-valley, single-band, single-Fermi-pocket parent state and is most likely a chiral topological pair-density wave, chara…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 026603] Published Fri Jan 16, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Chiho Yoon, Tianyi Xu, Yafis Barlas, and Fan Zhang

We investigate the recently discovered multiple superconducting states in rhombohedral graphene quarter metal. We demonstrate that one of these states features a single-spin, single-valley, single-band, single-Fermi-pocket parent state and is most likely a chiral topological pair-density wave, chara…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 026603] Published Fri Jan 16, 2026
item.dc:title: Quarter-Metal Superconductivity in Rhombohedral Graphene
item.dc:creator: Chiho Yoon, Tianyi Xu, Yafis Barlas, and Fan Zhang
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Supersymmetric Landau Levels in Subwavelength Type-I Dirac Metasurfaces
item.description:
Author(s): Yi Yuan, Yikai Xu, Liang Zhao, Qiong He, Shulin Sun, Shaojie Ma, and Lei Zhou

Dirac points, arising from intrinsic space-group symmetry in hexagonal lattices, have been extensively explored in graphene and photonic crystals. However, their characteristic scales, confined either to atomic level ($∼Å$) or limited by the operational wavelength ($∼λ$), pose fundamental challenges…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 023802] Published Thu Jan 15, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Yi Yuan, Yikai Xu, Liang Zhao, Qiong He, Shulin Sun, Shaojie Ma, and Lei Zhou

Dirac points, arising from intrinsic space-group symmetry in hexagonal lattices, have been extensively explored in graphene and photonic crystals. However, their characteristic scales, confined either to atomic level (∼Å) or limited by the operational wavelength (∼λ), pose fundamental challenges for…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 023802] Published Thu Jan 15, 2026
item.dc:title: Supersymmetric Landau Levels in Subwavelength Type-I Dirac Metasurfaces
item.dc:creator: Yi Yuan, Yikai Xu, Liang Zhao, Qiong He, Shulin Sun, Shaojie Ma, and Lei Zhou
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Atomic, Molecular, and Optical Physics

item.title: Microscopic Evidence of Spin-Driven Multiferroicity and Topological Spin Textures in Monolayer ${\mathrm{NiI}}_{2}$
item.description:
Author(s): Haitao Wang, Tianxing Jiang, Weiyi Pan, Xu Wang, Hongyu Wang, Junchao Tian, Lianchuang Li, Dongming Zhao, Qingle Zhang, Chenxi Wang, Ying Yang, Hongjun Xiang, Changsong Xu, Donglai Feng, and Tong Zhang

In type-II multiferroics, noncollinear spin textures are expected to induce electric polarization directly, leading to strong magnetoelectric coupling. Realizing such spin-driven multiferroicity in two-dimensional (2D) systems, and elucidating the interplay between local spins and electric polarizat…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 026402] Published Wed Jan 14, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Haitao Wang, Tianxing Jiang, Weiyi Pan, Xu Wang, Hongyu Wang, Junchao Tian, Lianchuang Li, Dongming Zhao, Qingle Zhang, Chenxi Wang, Ying Yang, Hongjun Xiang, Changsong Xu, Donglai Feng, and Tong Zhang

In type-II multiferroics, noncollinear spin textures are expected to induce electric polarization directly, leading to strong magnetoelectric coupling. Realizing such spin-driven multiferroicity in two-dimensional (2D) systems, and elucidating the interplay between local spins and electric polarizat…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 026402] Published Wed Jan 14, 2026
item.dc:title: Microscopic Evidence of Spin-Driven Multiferroicity and Topological Spin Textures in Monolayer ${\mathrm{NiI}}_{2}$
item.dc:creator: Haitao Wang, Tianxing Jiang, Weiyi Pan, Xu Wang, Hongyu Wang, Junchao Tian, Lianchuang Li, Dongming Zhao, Qingle Zhang, Chenxi Wang, Ying Yang, Hongjun Xiang, Changsong Xu, Donglai Feng, and Tong Zhang

item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: In-Plane Anomalous Features in the 3D Quantum Hall Regime
item.description:
Author(s): Ming Lu and Xiao-Xiao Zhang

Studies of the 3D quantum Hall effect (QHE) have primarily emphasized transport features that mimic the well-established 2D QHE. In this Letter, we show that qualitatively new features arise when an in-plane magnetic field is applied to a 3D Weyl semimetal in the quantum Hall regime. An unexpected H…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 026602] Published Wed Jan 14, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Ming Lu and Xiao-Xiao Zhang

Studies of the 3D quantum Hall effect (QHE) have primarily emphasized transport features that mimic the well-established 2D QHE. In this Letter, we show that qualitatively new features arise when an in-plane magnetic field is applied to a 3D Weyl semimetal in the quantum Hall regime. An unexpected H…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 026602] Published Wed Jan 14, 2026
item.dc:title: In-Plane Anomalous Features in the 3D Quantum Hall Regime
item.dc:creator: Ming Lu and Xiao-Xiao Zhang
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Ferromagnetic Interface Engineering of Spin-Charge Conversion in ${\mathrm{RuO}}_{2}$
item.description:
Author(s): Dongchao Yang, Zhaoqing Li, Yu Dai, Lili Lang, Zhong Shi, Zhe Yuan, and Shi-Ming Zhou

Spin-orbit torque efficiency is conventionally fixed by bulk materials. $D$-wave altermagnets introduce an additional nonrelativistic spin-charge conversion channel beyond the inverse spin-Hall effect. Using prototypical candidate ${\mathrm{RuO}}_{2}$ as an example, we show that the adjacent ferroma…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 026702] Published Wed Jan 14, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Dongchao Yang, Zhaoqing Li, Yu Dai, Lili Lang, Zhong Shi, Zhe Yuan, and Shi-Ming Zhou

Spin-orbit torque efficiency is conventionally fixed by bulk materials. D-wave altermagnets introduce an additional nonrelativistic spin-charge conversion channel beyond the inverse spin-Hall effect. Using prototypical candidate RuO2 as an example, we show that the adjacent ferromagnet alone can dic…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 026702] Published Wed Jan 14, 2026
item.dc:title: Ferromagnetic Interface Engineering of Spin-Charge Conversion in ${\mathrm{RuO}}_{2}$
item.dc:creator: Dongchao Yang, Zhaoqing Li, Yu Dai, Lili Lang, Zhong Shi, Zhe Yuan, and Shi-Ming Zhou
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Spontaneous Twirls and Structural Frustration in Moiré Materials
item.description:
Author(s): Jingtian Shi, Gaurav Chaudhary, Allan H. MacDonald, and Ivar Martin

Structural twirls form spontaneously in the domain wall networks of some moiré materials. We show that in heterobilayers, neighboring twirl chiralities tend to antialign, forming staggered patterns that are well described by antiferromagnetic lattice ${ϕ}^{4}$ theories. In moiré systems with triangu…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 026101] Published Tue Jan 13, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Jingtian Shi, Gaurav Chaudhary, Allan H. MacDonald, and Ivar Martin

Structural twirls form spontaneously in the domain wall networks of some moiré materials. We show that in heterobilayers, neighboring twirl chiralities tend to antialign, forming staggered patterns that are well described by antiferromagnetic lattice ϕ4 theories. In moiré systems with triangular dom…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 026101] Published Tue Jan 13, 2026
item.dc:title: Spontaneous Twirls and Structural Frustration in Moiré Materials
item.dc:creator: Jingtian Shi, Gaurav Chaudhary, Allan H. MacDonald, and Ivar Martin
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Altermagnet-Driven Magnon Spin Splitting Nernst Effect
item.description:
Author(s): Yuben Yang, Di Wang, Bin Yang, Peng Wang, Yuxuan Mu, Yuanzhe Tian, Bowen Zheng, Weijie Qin, Kaiyuan Wang, Biying Huang, Baigeng Wang, Xiangang Wan, and Di Wu

Magnonic spin current generation in antiferromagnets has emerged as an important topic in spintronics, where a strong external magnetic field or a Dzyaloshinskii-Moriya interaction (DMI) is generally required. Recently, a type of antiferromagnets characterized by momentum-dependent nonrelativistic s…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 026701] Published Mon Jan 12, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Yuben Yang, Di Wang, Bin Yang, Peng Wang, Yuxuan Mu, Yuanzhe Tian, Bowen Zheng, Weijie Qin, Kaiyuan Wang, Biying Huang, Baigeng Wang, Xiangang Wan, and Di Wu

Magnonic spin current generation in antiferromagnets has emerged as an important topic in spintronics, where a strong external magnetic field or a Dzyaloshinskii-Moriya interaction (DMI) is generally required. Recently, a type of antiferromagnets characterized by momentum-dependent nonrelativistic s…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 026701] Published Mon Jan 12, 2026
item.dc:title: Altermagnet-Driven Magnon Spin Splitting Nernst Effect
item.dc:creator: Yuben Yang, Di Wang, Bin Yang, Peng Wang, Yuxuan Mu, Yuanzhe Tian, Bowen Zheng, Weijie Qin, Kaiyuan Wang, Biying Huang, Baigeng Wang, Xiangang Wan, and Di Wu

item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Hybrid Quantum Surface Acoustic Wave with Skyrmion Qubit for Quantum Information Processing
item.description:
Author(s): Yu-Yuan Chen, Zhihui Peng, and Yu-xi Liu

Surface acoustic wave (SAW) devices are key components of classical communication systems and recently studied for quantum information processing. We here propose and study a hybrid quantum system composed of skyrmion qubit and a SAW cavity, which supports multiple long-lived phononic modes. The sys…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 013801] Published Tue Jan 06, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Yu-Yuan Chen, Zhihui Peng, and Yu-xi Liu

Surface acoustic wave (SAW) devices are key components of classical communication systems and recently studied for quantum information processing. We here propose and study a hybrid quantum system composed of skyrmion qubit and a SAW cavity, which supports multiple long-lived phononic modes. The sys…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 013801] Published Tue Jan 06, 2026
item.dc:title: Hybrid Quantum Surface Acoustic Wave with Skyrmion Qubit for Quantum Information Processing
item.dc:creator: Yu-Yuan Chen, Zhihui Peng, and Yu-xi Liu
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
